scenario: A DMN decides loan-rate adjustments based on the applicant's birthDate. The team wants to determine if the applicant was born between 1960-01-01 and 1980-12-31 (inclusive) for a specific rate offer. The team writes the input cell for the birthDate column as a range.
question: Which DMN range syntax expresses "between 1960-01-01 and 1980-12-31 inclusive"?

options.text: date("1980-12-31")] -- uses square brackets on both ends to indicate inclusive bounds in FEEL range notation. The date("YYYY-MM-DD") built-in function parses an ISO 8601 date string into a FEEL date value; without this conversion, direct string comparison would yield lexicographic ordering rather than chronological date comparison. This expression matches any birthDate value from January 1, 1960 through December 31, 1980 inclusive, exactly matching the requirement.

>= "1960-01-01" and <= "1980-12-31" -- uses comparison operators (>=, <=) with raw string literals. This approach has two problems: first, comparing birthDate (a date value) directly against string literals uses lexicographic string comparison rather than chronological date comparison, which may yield unexpected results for dates; second, Decision Table input cells conventionally use FEEL abbreviated range expressions, not the compound comparison form with explicit and.

between date("1960-01-01") and date("1980-12-31") -- uses a between keyword that might seem natural from SQL or other query languages. and keyword construct. b) for exclusive bounds. The between keyword is absent from the FEEL grammar and would cause a parse error in the DMN engine.

date("1980-12-31")) -- uses parentheses on both ends, which reverses the intended semantics. In FEEL range notation, parentheses indicate exclusive bounds (the endpoint value is excluded), while square brackets indicate inclusive bounds (the endpoint value is included). This expression would match birth dates strictly between January 1, 1960 and December 31, 1980, excluding both endpoints -- applicants born on exactly those boundary dates would not match, violating the requirement.

a.text: Correct. DMN/FEEL range syntax [a..b] with square brackets is inclusive on both bounds. For date ranges, use date("YYYY-MM-DD") to convert string literals to date type -- comparisons then work on dates. [date("1960-01-01")..date("1980-12-31")] matches any date from Jan 1, 1960 through Dec 31, 1980, inclusive.

b.text: Incorrect. Comparison operators with strings work in standalone FEEL but Decision Table input cells expect abbreviated FEEL forms; comparing strings without date() conversion also yields lexicographic comparison, not date comparison.

c.text: Incorrect. between is not standard DMN/FEEL syntax.

d.text: Incorrect. Parentheses are exclusive in DMN range notation, not inclusive.

explanation: DMN/FEEL range syntax [a..b] with square brackets is inclusive on both bounds. For date ranges, use date("YYYY-MM-DD") to convert string literals to date type -- comparisons then work on dates. [date("1960-01-01")..date("1980-12-31")] matches any date from Jan 1, 1960 through Dec 31, 1980, inclusive.
